text.title: Insight Evidence Card Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Insight Evidence Card Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each card pins ONE atomic claim. Compound claims ('users want X and Y') are split into two cards.
rule.rationale: Compound claims defy evidence aggregation; atomic cards make confidence calculable.

rule.statement: Each card carries the strongest verbatim quote backing the claim. Paraphrases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Paraphrase drift turns evidence into opinion within two synthesis cycles.

rule.statement: Each card pins evidence_count = number of distinct interviews that surface the claim. Claims with count=1 are tagged 'anecdotal'; claims with count≥3 can feed opportunity tree.
rule.rationale: Count-based gating prevents single-interview anecdotes from inflating the tree.

rule.statement: Each card names the segment the claim applies to. 'All users' is rejected unless the segment map has a single segment.
rule.rationale: Cross-segment claims hide where the claim breaks; segment tagging makes the claim falsifiable.

rule.statement: Each card carries a confidence band (low / medium / high) keyed to evidence_count + recency + interviewer diversity.
rule.rationale: Without confidence band stakeholders default to high-confidence reading.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
